hypothesis: A Transformer encoder–decoder using patch-based spectrum embeddings and a jointly trained spectral reconstruction alignment network, pretrained on simulated MD-derived IR spectra and fine-tuned on NIST spectra with multitask concentration training, can accurately predict constituent molecular structures from binary and ternary mixture IR spectra (achieving high Top-k accuracy and Tanimoto similarity on simulated and experimental ATR-IR mixtures) and outperforms architectures lacking the alignment/reconstruction loss.
